name: Embalse del Atazar – complete loop from El Berrueco – gravel
name_original: Embalse del Atazar – vuelta completa desde El Berrueco – gravel
region_intro: Touring cycling around El Berrueco offers routes through the Sierra Norte de Madrid, characterized by pine-covered mountains, deep ravines, and peaceful roads. The region features the expansive El Atazar Reservoir, a central natural attraction with numerous cycling routes along its shores. Granite formations, including "berrocales" and "piedras caballeras," define the landscape of the nearby Sierra de La Cabrera. The area provides varied terrain, from gravel paths to narrow roads with minimal traffic, suitable for different cycling preferences. Best touring cycling routes around El Berrueco The most popular touring cycling route is Embalse del Atazar – complete loop from El Berrueco – gravel, a 37.1 miles (59.7 km) trail that takes 5 hours 35 minutes to complete. This route offers a full circuit around the El Atazar Reservoir on gravel paths. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Views of the Lozoya River – El Atazar Reservoir loop from El Berrueco, a difficult 27.0 miles (43.4 km) path. This route provides scenic views of the Lozoya River and the El Atazar Reservoir. Local touring cyclists also love the View of El Atazar Reservoir – Torrelaguna loop from El Berrueco, a 26.3 miles (42.4 km) trail leading through the reservoir area and towards Torrelaguna, often completed in about 2 hours 49 minutes. Touring cycling around El Berrueco is defined by routes around the El Atazar Reservoir, through the Sierra Norte de Madrid, and past granite formations. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy paths to challenging mountain routes. The routes in El Berrueco are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.75 stars from over 4 reviews. More than 190 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore El Berrueco's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around El Berrueco?
A: There are 17 touring cycling routes around El Berrueco, offering diverse options for exploring the Sierra Norte de Madrid.

Q: What kind of difficulty can I expect on touring cycling routes in El Berrueco?
A: The routes around El Berrueco cater to various skill levels. You'll find 1 easy route, 4 moderate routes, and 12 more challenging routes, many of which feature significant elevation changes and gravel sections around the El Atazar Reservoir.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes around El Berrueco?
A: Yes, many routes in the area are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e959844782/embalse-del-atazar-complete-loop-from-el-berrueco-gravel" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse del Atazar – complete loop from El Berrueco – gravel&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a full circuit around the reservoir.

Q: What are some scenic viewpoints or landmarks I can visit while cycling in El Berrueco?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty and historical sites. You can enjoy stunning views of the El Atazar Reservoir, visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3080222" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puentes Viejas Dam&lt;/a&gt;, or explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3274718" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Berrueco Watchtower&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2791690" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Villar Reservoir and Gorge&lt;/a&gt; also offers picturesque scenery.

Q: Are there any family-friendly touring cycling routes in El Berrueco?
A: While many routes are challenging, there is at least one easy touring cycling route available. For families, consider routes with less elevation gain and smoother surfaces, often found closer to the reservoir's flatter sections, though specific family-focused routes are not detailed in the provided data.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in El Berrueco?
A: El Berrueco, located in the Sierra Norte de Madrid, is generally pleasant for cycling during spring and autumn when temperatures are milder. Summers can be hot, especially around the reservoir, while winters can be cold with potential for snow in higher elevations. Always check local weather conditions before your ride.

Q: What are some longer touring cycling routes available in the area?
A: For a longer challenge, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31507150" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of El Atazar Reservoir – Puentes Viejas Dam loop from El Berrueco&lt;/a&gt;, which covers over 123 km and involves significant elevation, offering an extensive exploration of the reservoir and surrounding dams.

Q: Can I find routes that offer views of the Lozoya River?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14887857" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of the Lozoya River – El Atazar Reservoir loop from El Berrueco&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 43.4 km route specifically designed to provide scenic views of the Lozoya River as it flows into the El Atazar Reservoir.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the touring cycling routes around El Berrueco?
A: The terrain varies significantly, from peaceful narrow roads with minimal traffic to gravel paths, especially around the El Atazar Reservoir. The region also features granite formations and pine-covered mountains, offering a mix of surfaces and landscapes.

Q: Are there any routes that connect El Berrueco with nearby towns like Torrelaguna?
A: Yes, you can cycle towards nearby towns. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32012300" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of El Atazar Reservoir – Torrelaguna loop from El Berrueco&lt;/a&gt; is a 42.4 km route that takes you through the reservoir area and towards Torrelaguna, offering a connection to another local settlement.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in El Berrueco?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 3.75 stars. Cyclists often praise the diverse landscapes, the stunning views of the El Atazar Reservoir, and the peaceful roads through the Sierra Norte de Madrid.

Q: Are there any routes that specifically focus on the El Atazar Reservoir?
A: Many routes in the area feature the El Atazar Reservoir prominently. Beyond the complete loop, another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6929269" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Atazar Reservoir – Views of the Lozoya River loop from El Berrueco&lt;/a&gt;, which offers additional perspectives of this central natural attraction.
tips: It is the largest reservoir in Madrid. There is a viewpoint that is at a height above the wall of the dam from where you can see the development of the reservoir and its tail.
Ideal to spend a night indoors. The floor is on land and has neither door nor windows.

A good section along the forest track on the Senda del Genaro, it has open areas where you can see the slate landscape and the vegetation. In summer it is pleasant to walk through the wooded areas and enjoy the shade.
This dam offers spectacular views. It is worth going there. Ideal for hiking, motorbike or road bike excursions. Next to the dam there is a small parking lot for about 10 cars.
Every year in this typical town with granite construction, a living nativity scene is held in style. The neighbors dress up and represent different scenes of the Christian religious tradition.
